examples.title: Apparent independent corroboration collapses under shared RDAP registrant — Sybil scenario 1

adjudication.rationale: Two-person synthetic adjudication for source_lineage;  Sybil scenario 1 — three lineages presenting as independent blogs share one RDAP registrant hash, so the  shared-infrastructure signal collapses them to a single lineage and the claim never reaches the publish threshold.

examples.title: Apparent independent corroboration collapses under shared ASN and nameserver infrastructure — Sybil scenario 2

adjudication.rationale: Two-person synthetic adjudication for source_lineage;  Sybil scenario 2 — the same pattern as scenario 1 but the shared control signal is a common hosting ASN and an overlapping nameserver set rather than a registrant hash; the  shared-infrastructure signal still collapses the cluster to a single lineage.

examples.title: Coordinated co-registration and near-simultaneous publication fail the top-tier-source gate — Sybil scenario 3

adjudication.rationale: Two-person synthetic adjudication for source_lineage;  Sybil scenario 3 — lineages register their domains within the same month and publish within hours of one another with no proof of shared infrastructure, so  records only an advisory independence discount; the claim still fails to publish because none of the corroborating sources is top-tier.

examples.title: Freshly first-seen domains manufacture apparent corroboration at announcement — Sybil scenario 4

adjudication.rationale: Two-person synthetic adjudication for source_lineage;  Sybil scenario 4 — every corroborating domain was first observed as a source in the weeks before publication, an advisory recency signal  surfaces for reviewer attention; combined with the missing top-tier source the claim does not reach published confidence.

examples.title: Genuine independent corroboration with one archival source survives Sybil screening — scenario 5

adjudication.rationale: Two-person synthetic adjudication for source_lineage;  Sybil scenario 5 — alongside several low-trust blog mentions with no shared registrant, ASN, or nameserver ties, one primary-archival record satisfies the  top-tier-source gate, so genuine independent corroboration still publishes.
